title: youngia japonica subsp. elstonii
intro: Youngia japonica subsp. elstonii, also known as Elston's ragwort or Japanese chrysanthemum, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral activities. The health benefits associated with Youngia japonica subsp. elstonii include its ability to alleviate fever, treat wounds, and support digestive health. Youngia japonica subsp. elstonii is typically prepared for medicinal use through herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures.
